data.body:
==================== gnu-whale-ichor — CURRENT STATE + DELIVERED-CODE MAP (handoff pt2) ====================
--- ENV ---
Worktree: /Users/joeoakhart/rebar-identity  branch: identity-epic  (local venv built: source .venv/bin/activate).
Based on origin/main (was rebased forward once mid-run to fix a stale-base CI failure — the
`scripts/check_docs_index.py` docs-index gate was missing from the old base). commit-msg hook installed
(shared .git/hooks). Gerrit remote = `gerrit` (RebarBotNava). All gates ON (plan-review-for-claim,
completion-verification-for-close, ticket-for-commit).

--- GERRIT STACK (8 changes, relation chain; ALL currently LLM-Review+1 AND Verified+1 — but DIVERGED, DO NOT LAND) ---
558 ddbe (entity)            | 560 4e9d (attribution)     | 561 bde5 (authorship — DIVERGED, TS1)
563 e165 (key lifecycle — DIVERGED, TS2) | 576 264f (Jira outbound) | 579 2f13 (inbound ghost)
580 3183 (enforcement — DIVERGED, TS3)    | 581 4b5b (docs — DIVERGED, TS4)
Stack order: ddbe->4e9d->bde5->e165->264f->2f13->3183->4b5b. NOTE: bde5/e165 (to rework) are MID-stack.

--- TICKET STATES ---
CLOSED (signed plan-review+completion-verifier): ddbe, 4e9d, bde5, e165, 264f(primary-lipophobic-dugong),
  2f13(base-doggish-barracuda). bde5/e165 are CLOSED but their DESIGN is being transitioned (TS1/TS2) —
  they may need reopen for the rework, OR the rework lands under the transition stories.
CLAIMED but NOT closed (their completion-verifier would pass, but design diverged): 3183, 4b5b.
EPIC e68d: in_progress, NOT closed (completion-verifier FAILED on AC8 ledger-schema + AC9 verdict — correct).

--- KEY DELIVERED-CODE LOCATIONS (what to rework) ---
- Authorship: src/rebar/attest/authorship.py (sign_authorship/verify_authorship/verify_authorship_at_epoch/
  epoch_for_position/keyop_payload/resolve_trust_root; POLICY[rebar.authorship.v1]=sshsig registered in attest/__init__.py).
- Event signing + write-gate: src/rebar/_commands/_seam.py (author_sig stamping + attribution_fields cache + UX write-gate).
- Keyring/era (HLC-epoch): src/rebar/reducer/_processors.py (process_key_event, keyring_epoch, genesis bootstrap,
  snapshot freeze/restore), _state.py (seeds keyring/keyring_epoch), _version.py (KEY_ADD/KEY_REVOKE, SCHEMA_VERSION),
  _cache.py (_REDUCER_CACHE_VERSION), _store/event_append.py (EVENT_TYPES + invalid-type error string).
- Key lifecycle seam: src/rebar/_commands/identity.py (add_identity_key/revoke_identity_key, resolve_current_identity,
  resolve_mapping/jira_account_id/identity_email/ensure_identity_for/is_placeholder, identity CLI intercept).
- Merge-gate: src/rebar/_commands/verify_authorship.py (CLI verify-authorship, classify verified/unsigned/
  unknown-author/bad-signature, _collect_all skips gate-exempt types, reads snapshot authorship_ledger).
- Ledger: src/rebar/_commands/compact.py (_build_authorship_ledger in _compact_locked + rebuild_snapshot_from_full_log).
- Config: src/rebar/_config_schema.py ([identity] require_authenticated + signing_key; REBAR_IDENTITY_* env).
- fsck authorship line: src/rebar/_commands/fsck.py. Jira reconciler: outbound_differ.py/dispatch_one.py/acli.py/
  acli_rest.py/outbound_fields.py (264f — NOT changing). Docs: docs/identity.md + docs/adr/0045 (TS4).
- Tests: tests/unit/test_identity_<leaf>.py (+_heldout) and tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/test_identity_264f_*.py,
  test_identity_2f13_inbound.py. Held-out oracle files were kept in $CLAUDE_JOB_DIR/tmp/heldout during dispatch.

--- KEY IMPLEMENTATION DECISIONS (delivered) ---
- identity type registered at ~9 exempt sites (schema enum->gen types.py via `python -m rebar.schemas.gen_types`;
  _TYPES/_FILE_IMPACT_EXEMPT_TYPES/_NON_GRAPH_ARTIFACT_TYPES/EXCLUDED_SYNC_TYPES/_GATE_EXEMPT_TYPES; claim+close gate
  tuples; plan_review orchestrator+workflow_ops). identity CLI = main() intercept in _cli/__init__.py (NOT the
  golden-file dispatcher, to avoid touching tests/golden). create_identity is a CANONICAL MCP tool (registered in
  the tests/interfaces/facades/test_mcp_output_schema_coverage.py ledger).
- acli.py must NOT import rebar core (engine/core layering) — Jira resolution lives in the flow layer.
- outbound_differ.py + dispatch_one.py grandfathered into .github/module-size-allowlist.txt + ceilings +
  docs/architecture.md remedy rows (they crossed 800 LOC for 264f). fsck.py held at exactly 800.
- gen_cli_reference.py INTERCEPT_COMMANDS + tests/unit/test_gen_cli_reference.py _LADDER include "identity";
  docs/cli-reference.md + docs/mcp-reference.md regenerated (in the ddbe commit).

--- OPERATIONAL CONSTRAINTS FOR THE RESUMER ---
- `git rebase -i` is UNAVAILABLE. To amend a mid-stack commit: git checkout <sha>; apply change; git commit --amend;
  git rebase --onto <new-sha> <old-sha> identity-epic. (Used repeatedly this run.)
- Plan-review of a stacked story that depends on unmerged ancestors MUST use `rebar review-plan <id> --ref HEAD`
  (else it reviews origin/main and false-flags missing deps). Sign on every passing round.
- The completion-verifier is STRICT per-AC: it demands a TEST asserting EACH AC clause (it caught under-asserting
  tests on ddbe/e165, a self-defeating merge-gate that didn't exempt identity tickets, and missing per-AC tests
  for 264f reporter/user-search + 2f13 inbound — all backfilled). Write one test per AC clause.
- CI: gerrit-verify runs docs-index/module-size/gen-cli/gen-mcp/env-registry drift gates BEYOND `make test`; the
  local pre-flight `make lint && make typecheck && make test` does NOT cover the drift gates — run
  `python scripts/check_docs_index.py --check`, `gen_cli_reference.py`, `gen_mcp_reference.py`, `gen_env_registry.py`
  too. Known FLAKE: test_resolve_symbol_confirms_third_party_module (yaml stdlib classification) fails locally on
  clean main — ignore. macOS runner OSError 'could not create numbered dir' basetemp flake -> comment `recheck`.
- Held-out TDD per rebar-implement: orchestrator authors happy-path (implementer sees) + edge/E2E (held out); mutation-check for teeth.
- NEXT ACTION: work TS1 c96d first (review-plan --ref HEAD, claim, held-out TDD, amend change 561), then TS2/TS3/TS4,
  then re-run epic completion-verifier, then submit the corrected stack.
